{{Infobox book
| name = The Pilot
| slug = lh-04-the-pilot

| follows = [[The Knight (novel)|The Knight]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-lh-04-the-pilot.jpg|250px]]
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The story follows the interconnected lives of Combat Artist{{!}}Combat Artists, pilots, and political leaders caught in a galactic struggle against the mysterious organization [[Solstice]]. The narrative begins with [[Aila]], an experienced [[Combat Artist]] who is transported to a frozen planet where she accepts a challenge from the sentient [[Zenith Blade]]. Simultaneously, [[Benri Vallenar]] is drawn into [[Solstice]]'s orbit on the planet Fathom, where he learns of the threat posed by his son, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic, and the The Zenith Starship{{!}}Zenith Starship. As the Captain of *[[The Last Horizon]]* attempts to dismantle [[Solstice]] and find her father, she faces escalating violence, including bioweapon attacks and the awakening of powerful [[Zenith Devices]] like the [[Void Prism]].

Flashbacks reveal a tragic history: 150 years prior, Omal{{!}}Omal Escalon underwent dangerous [[Aethertech]] augmentations to save his daughter, [[Aila]], from the [[Keeper virus]]. Driven by a vengeful obsession to destroy [[Solstice]], [[Omal]]’s actions set in motion a cycle of violence that eventually leads to his daughter [[Aila]] becoming a formidable combatant wielding the [[Zenith Blade]]. In the present, these historical threads converge as [[Aila]] attacks *[[The Last Horizon]]*, leading to a brutal confrontation between her and the pilot, [[Omega]]. During these battles, the crew of *[[The Last Horizon]]* struggles with moral dilemmas, particularly after [[Omega]] is forced to fire on a civilian militia to escape an encirclement.

As the conflict intensifies, [[Benri Vallenar]] works with [[Solstice]] to capture a data core containing [[Karoshan]] research, unaware that the organization is backed by the terrifying [[World Spirits]]. A massive ritual on [[Zykaros]] successfully manifests a copy of the [[Iron King]], but it triggers a massacre by the [[World Spirits]], leading to [[Benri]]'s assassination and subsequent revival in stasis. The scale of the war expands as [[Commander]] Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax attempts to rally [[Karoshan forces]], only to be betrayed by her brother [[Veshran]] and cast into the vacuum of space.

The climax reaches a fever pitch on the toxic planet [[Zykaros]]. The Captain and her crew attempt to hunt [[Solstice]]'s leadership, only to discover the horrifying truth: [[Solstice]] is composed of [[World Spirits]]. In a desperate attempt to survive, [[Omega]] sacrifices his remaining life force to save the dying Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax, effectively dying in the process. The story concludes in total chaos; the Captain is forced into a [[binding]] contract with the [[World Spirits]] Fathom and [[Zykaros]], which triggers a catastrophic release of curse magic. This event infects the entire galaxy, leaving it in a state of global ruin as mysterious Shadow{{!}}shadow entities begin making new deals with the [[Zenith Chamber]].}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aila]], an experienced [[Combat Artist]], spends a week performing relief efforts in the [[Visiria system]] by hunting [[D’Niss]] spawn and rescuing survivors. After discovering a [[Subline]] report featuring her grandfather, she attempts to return home but is intercepted by mysterious yellow sparks that hijack her ship and transport her to a frozen planet. There, she accepts a challenge from the sentient [[Zenith Blade]] to compete against other Combat Artist{{!}}Combat Artists for control of the weapon.}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Benri Vallenar]] travels to the planet Fathom, where his ship is covertly diverted by the mysterious organization [[Solstice]] to a remote hangar. There, he joins a high-level meeting of masked leaders via magical contract and holographic projection to discuss the threat posed by his son, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic, and the The Zenith Starship{{!}}Zenith Starship. As [[Benri]] offers his expertise to help combat the starship, an unexpected intruder named [[Omega]] reveals themselves, having infiltrated the meeting by hiding behind one of the attendees.}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|After successfully capturing three [[Solstice]] recruits through coordinated attacks by [[Omega]], [[Sola]], and [[Mell]], the Captain's [[pathfinding magic]] is blocked by unknown mystical protections. The situation escalates when a [[Zenith Device]] known as [[Void Prism]] contacts the ship, revealing that multiple such powerful entities are awakening. Despite a tense negotiation where [[Prism]] refuses to ally with the Captain, she remains determined to dismantle [[Solstice]] and find her father.}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|After using [[contract magic]] to interrogate [[Doctor Oladyne]], the protagonist and their crew capture a high-ranking Vice Chief of Planetary Security{{!}}Vice Chief who claims to be protected by [[Solstice]]. The situation turns deadly when [[Solstice]] deploys a bioweapon and magic-dispelling mist via a hijacked civilian shuttle, resulting in the Vice Chief of Planetary Security{{!}}Vice Chief's death. Following the skirmish, [[Omega]] reveals that [[Solstice]] is systematically gathering specialists in fabrication and mind magic, prompting the Captain to order a widespread investigation into the organization.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|While traveling on a ship, [[Benri Vallenar]] uses a magical clone to interrogate [[Nila]], who reveals she was ordered by the mysterious organization [[Solstice]] to report on him and guide him to [[Dornoth]]. [[Nila]] expresses deep fear of [[Solstice]]'s reach and power, but [[Benri]] uses the information to confirm his destination. After the interrogation, [[Benri]]'s true self receives the data and orders his heavily armed warship to [[Dornoth]] to confront the organization on his own terms.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Ambassador [[Tythalia]] advises Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax to launch an immediate attack against [[Felrex]], noting that [[Karoshan refugees]] are becoming discouraged by the negative reputation of [[The Last Horizon]]. Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax rejects this advice, choosing to maintain her honor and wait for the right moment despite her limited fleet. Following a warning from the mage [[Melerius]] regarding potential indirect threats to her family, Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax decides to recall her children to the safety of [[Shadow Ark]].}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Varic Vallenar{{!}}Captain Vallenar arrives on the cavern-planet of [[Basyrryx]] to find the Karoshan{{!}}Karoshans rapidly constructing a new city using advanced fabricators. While observing the progress, Vallenar conducts a magic lesson for [[Prince Rellask]] and [[Princess Nasharia]], using Aetheric tests to help them discover their elemental inclinations. During the lesson, Vallenar shares personal insights about magic and expresses a deep, intense disdain for Alchemists{{!}}alchemists.}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|After [[Mell]] discovers suspicious data on the Vice Chief of Planetary Security{{!}}Vice Chief's console, the Captain uses magic to identify a lead regarding a security consultation at an uninhabited moon. The two then enter the ship's high-security [[vault]], which contains various dangerous relics and the deactivated [[Starhammer]]. Using a pathfinding spell, the Captain helps [[Mell]] identify new components within [[Starhammer]]'s body created by the [[Evolution Engine]], providing her with hope that she can repair him.}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|After [[Horizon]] nudges the Captain to mentor her student mages, the Captain discovers them working together to enchant a new training room aboard the ship. During the encounter, the Captain reveals that their mission to [[Dornoth]] involves fighting against elements of the [[Galactic Union]], forcing the students to contemplate their loyalties. Following the meeting, a paranoid Captain instructs [[Horizon]] to monitor the students' communications for any signs of infiltration.}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The Captain and their crew investigate a suspicious presence in the [[Dornoth system]], discovering an experimental [[Vallenar Corporation]] battlecruiser belonging to the Captain's father. While monitoring the ship, the crew uses advanced scanners to realize that the core components of the [[Grand Hive]] have been stolen from the system. The crew concludes that someone is likely harvesting the massive amount of biotech and raw materials to potentially create a new [[Iron King]].}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|After discovering a mysterious note and [[Iron Legion]] technology on a space station, [[Benri Vallenar]] is contacted by the secret organization [[Solstice]]. An anonymous voice informs him that he has passed an initiation by demonstrating his magical abilities and provides coordinates to [[Visiria]]. The chapter concludes with the [[Vallenar Corporation]] receiving an unexpected shipment containing a reconstructed [[Iron Bishop]].}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|After being scanned by an unknown [[Aethertech]] presence, the crew of [[The Last Horizon]] attempts to intercept a Vallenar ship but is repelled by a massive orange [[force-blade]] strike. The attacker is revealed to be [[Aila]], the wielder of the [[Zenith Blade]], who engages in a tense verbal confrontation with [[Omega]]. The chapter concludes as [[Aila]] attacks the ship, initiating a battle.}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Set 156 years in the past, Omal{{!}}Omal Escalon visits a hospital where his daughter, [[Aila]], is undergoing experimental augmentations to combat the aggressive [[Keeper virus]]. While the augmentations grant her superhuman potential, the doctors warn that the virus is evolving to keep pace with her healing. In an effort to help his daughter, [[Omal]] impulsively volunteers to become a test subject for the rare [[Aethertech]] modifications himself.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|As [[Aila Escalon]]'s devastating attacks shred the ship's shields, the crew manages to teleport the erratic [[Pilot]], [[Omega]], away from the bridge. To counter [[Aila]]'s ability to create multiple clones via her [[Zenith Device]], Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic uses a magic spell to duplicate [[Sola]], allowing her to launch a coordinated temporal and cursed attack. The battle concludes with the destruction of most of [[Aila]]'s clones, leaving only the [[Zenith Blade]] drifting in space as a new threat emerges.}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|As the protagonist's fleet faces a combined assault from [[Aila Escalon]] and a [[Lichborn fleet]], their defensive magic is dismantled by a targeted counterspell. [[Aila]] uses the [[Zenith Blade]] to strike [[Shadow Ark]], inadvertently dragging the ship into [[Subspace]] and leaving the pilot, [[Omega]], stranded inside the vessel. While the crew struggles to manage the [[Subspace dive]], [[Omega]] realizes he has been captured within [[Shadow Ark]] by his own daughter and her allies.}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Following a battle where [[Aila Escalon]] and the [[Zenith Processor]] successfully captured the [[Shadow Ark]], [[Aila]] reflects on her decision to prioritize saving lives over destroying her father's ship. Meanwhile, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Captain Vallenar of [[The Last Horizon]] is forced into a magical contract with the [[Voice of Solstice]] to destroy a [[Perfected]] research facility in exchange for the release of their hostages. Although Vallenar agrees to the deal to buy time, she and her crew secretly resolve to kill their captors once they have secured the safety of their comrades.}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Set 154 years in the past, Omal{{!}}Omal Escalon and his criminals corner [[Doctor Elios]] and his assistant [[Herta]] in a hospital observation room. After exposing their lies regarding [[Herta]]'s true expertise in viral engineering and their secret communications, [[Omal]] reveals that the pair were working for an unknown entity to study his daughter's augmentations. [[Herta]] claims they were seeking a way to achieve immortality, prompting an enraged [[Omal]] to threaten them with retribution.}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Benri Vallenar]] and his crew successfully escape Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic by entering [[Subspace]], eventually docking on [[Visiria]] where [[Benri]] receives a mysterious, compressed delivery from [[Solstice]]. Meanwhile, [[Raion]] and [[Omega]] find themselves held in custody aboard a [[Divine Titan hangar]] after being captured. To scout their surroundings, [[Raion]] and [[Doctor Cryce]] prepare a maintenance drone, which [[Omega]] uses as a vessel to infiltrate the ship's systems from within [[Subspace]].}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The narrator visits Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax to receive a file containing information about her brother, [[Veshran]], who may help them navigate toward their next objective. During the meeting, Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax expresses deep concern regarding whether the contract with [[Solstice]] can be trusted, prompting the narrator to promise they will break it if it is used to cause harm. After discussing their preparations and the potential for enemy interference, Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax sends the narrator back to continue enchanting equipment and reinforcing the crew's readiness.}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The crew of the The Last Horizon{{!}}Last Horizon infiltrates a [[Perfected]]-controlled research station to steal data regarding horrific biological experiments, including attempts to create an [[Iron King]] and hybrids with [[Subspace]] entities. After [[Mell]] successfully retrieves the data core, the team discovers the extent of the atrocities and prepares to destroy the station. The mission is interrupted when a distant fleet, led by [[Prince Veshran]] of [[Karosha]], obliterates the outpost and demands to speak with Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax.}}
* '''Chapter 21:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax and her bodyguard, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic, confront [[Prince Veshran]] on his battleship to request military support for retrieving hostages. The meeting becomes a heated political dispute as [[Veshran]] insults Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax's leadership and Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic's status, revealing deep tensions within the [[Karoshan]] court. Ultimately, Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax asserts her Authority{{!}}authority and directs [[Veshran]] to prepare for a mission to [[Union space]] to recover their people.}}
* '''Chapter 22:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|After returning to Fathom, [[Vallenar Corporation]] President [[Benri]] meets with holographic representatives from [[Solstice]], who task him with retrieving a data core from his son, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic. The data core contains [[Karoshan]] research regarding the [[Iron Legion]], which [[Solstice]] intends to repurpose as a galactic asset. [[Benri]] agrees to secure the package while expressing interest in seeing their plan, despite his skepticism regarding their goals.}}
* '''Chapter 23:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The seven [[Zenith Devices]] awaken simultaneously in a digital void, leading to a tense meeting filled with bickering and conflicting alliances. [[Void Prism]] proposes that the [[Aether]] has awakened them all to face an unprecedented galactic threat, a theory supported by [[Genesis Vault]]'s claim that the fundamental nature of the [[Aether]] shifted recently. Despite [[Prism]]'s attempt to organize them, the siblings fail to reach an agreement and log out individually, leaving [[Horizon]] alone to contemplate the looming danger.}}
* '''Chapter 24:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|After infiltrating a secure cargo ship by possessing a cleaning robot, [[Omega]] hijacks the vessel and begins a chaotic takeover of its bridge. Meanwhile, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic travels to the planet Fathom to deliver a data core to a clone of his father as part of a tense negotiation with the [[Zenith Processor]]. The chapter concludes as [[Omega]] successfully pilots the hijacked ship back to Fathom, reuniting with Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic's crew.}}
* '''Chapter 25:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|After [[Omega]] confirms he has captured the hostages, the Captain kills a clone of his father and prepares to defend himself against the [[Zenith Processor]] on Fathom. Meanwhile, Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax coordinates a complex space battle involving [[Omega]] and her crew to disrupt the pursuing [[Union fleet]]. The conflict culminates in Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax performing a daring space maneuver that destroys [[Omega]]'s ship, successfully releasing two [[Divine Titans]] to drive back the enemy forces.}}
* '''Chapter 26:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|As the The Zenith Starship{{!}}Zenith Starship attacks the [[Galactic Union]]'s capital, Fathom, the galaxy reacts with shock and widespread support for the [[Union]]. Onboard [[The Last Horizon]], mage [[Mariala Brechess]] struggles with moral uncertainty while the ship's [[World Spirit]] instructs her and other mages to break a sealing spell to facilitate teleportation. Meanwhile, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Professor Varic Vallenar fights a desperate, solitary defensive battle against overwhelming [[Union]] forces in a starship hangar.}}
* '''Chapter 27:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Governor [[Nelula Seiren]] of [[Visiria]] decides to officially condemn [[The Last Horizon]] and forbid their members from entering her system to avoid a personal conflict with [[Varic Vallenar]]. Meanwhile, at [[Advocate headquarters]], [[High Councilor]] [[Blade Dancer]] fails to secure support for sending aid to Fathom, though the new councilor [[Leilari]] secretly sends a private message. On Fathom, a civilian militia of volunteers begins rushing into orbit to defend their planet against the The Zenith Starship{{!}}Zenith Starship.}}
* '''Chapter 28:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|After being cornered by the [[Galactic Union]], the narrator is rescued by their students, who provide defensive magic long enough for the group to seize a starship and escape into orbit. Once safely aboard the The Zenith Starship{{!}}Zenith Starship, the narrator realizes their allies have effectively declared war on the [[Union]] to save them. As the fleet attempts to navigate a magical path through the enemy encirclement, they are suddenly intercepted by [[Aila Escalon]] and the [[Zenith Blade]].}}
* '''Chapter 29:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|During a chaotic space battle, [[Omega]] engages in a fierce duel with his daughter [[Aila]], who uses her [[Zenith Blade]] and advanced augmentations to match him move for move. After a brutal confrontation in [[Subspace]], [[Omega]] realizes his crew is being trapped by [[Solstice]] using civilian ships as human shields. To escape the encirclement, [[Omega]] fires the full power of [[The Last Horizon]], breaking the enemy formation but destroying the civilian militia in the process.}}
* '''Chapter 30:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|After escaping the defenders of Fathom with the help of [[Lemon]] and her Nova-Bots{{!}}Nova-Bot crew, the protagonist's team holds a tense meeting to address [[Omega]]'s decision to fire on a civilian fleet. While [[Sola]] and [[Mell]] condemn [[Omega]] as a psychotic killer, the protagonist defends his logic as a necessary tactic against [[Solstice]]'s use of human shields. The confrontation ends with an emotional outburst from [[Omega]], who flees into [[Subspace]] after being offered a sympathetic hug by [[Raion]].}}
* '''Chapter 31:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|While practicing [[pathfinding magic]] to counter [[Void Prism]], the narrator meets with six students to thank them for saving his life and warn them that their families are in danger due to their association with him. To protect the students, he provides them with a file of information to report to the [[Galactic Union]] and gifts them replica mantles and amulets for protection. He concludes by promising that once his conflict with [[Solstice]] is resolved, they may return to the ship as part of his crew.}}
* '''Chapter 32:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Benri Vallenar]] meets with the [[Voice of Solstice]], promising to provide data from a recovered core and investigate the crew of [[The Last Horizon]] in exchange for a meeting with [[Solstice]]'s leaders. Meanwhile, [[Sola]] visits the [[Titan Force]] hangar to check on her equipment and speaks with [[Mell]] and [[Doctor Cryce]]. While there, [[Sola]] receives distressing messages from family and her old friend [[Ortila]], who condemn her for her recent actions and the trouble she has brought upon them.}}
* '''Chapter 33:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Set 152 years ago, [[Omal]] celebrates killing four more members of the corrupt organization known as [[Solstice]] by pinning their pictures to his wall. His daughter, [[Aila]], expresses concern that his obsessive hunt for the group is neglecting their family business and will never truly end. Despite [[Aila]]'s desire to help him once she graduates, [[Omal]] remains driven by vengeance and is willing to sacrifice even her to achieve his goal.}}
* '''Chapter 34:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|As the crew of [[The Last Horizon]] faces galaxy-wide backlash, their identities are exposed and their connections—including students, relatives, and business interests—are targeted through harassment, theft, and sabotage. While [[Lutoch Terelle]] and the [[Void Prism]] analyze this data to systematically dismantle the crew's lives, [[Solstice]] remains preoccupied with personal concerns regarding [[Starhammer]] and [[Omega]]. Meanwhile, the narrator performs a multi-day ritual using magical artifacts to search for a specific destination, eventually successfully locating [[Solstice]] through golden threads of magic.}}
* '''Chapter 35:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The Captain reveals that they have performed a ritual to trace the wide-scale connections and information thefts coordinated by [[Solstice]]. While the enemy remains concealed, the Captain presents a map of various contacts and paths that lead back toward Fathom. The group agrees to divide tasks, using their unique skills and resources to follow these connections and hunt down the enemy's agents.}}
* '''Chapter 36:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] uses [[Subspace]] to transport [[Lemon]] to a nearby planet so she can reunite with a [[Lichborn woman]], leaving him in a dark and cynical mood. Upon returning to his ship, [[Omega]] eavesdrops on [[Commander]] Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax as she discusses political instability and a lack of trust from her brother, [[Veshran]]. The conversation turns to their shared experiences with betrayal and vengeance, ultimately prompting [[Omega]] to abandon his melancholy in favor of pursuing more violent distractions.}}
* '''Chapter 37:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The narrator tracks down a man named [[Gunder]] on the desert planet [[Huun]], discovering he sold a class list from the [[Magic Tower]] to an entity known as 'Oracle of Solstice{{!}}The Oracle of Solstice.' Later, while searching for leads on [[Dornoth One]], [[Sola]]'s contact is killed in a targeted explosion at her shop. After [[Sola]] captures the suspected attacker, the narrator uses [[pathfinding magic]] and digital hacking to reveal the girl is a repeat thief of [[Aethertech]] before they escape the angry crowd.}}
* '''Chapter 38:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Benri]], using an anonymous clone to protect his identity, travels to a hidden island on the industrial planet [[Zykaros]] to meet with the mysterious organization [[Solstice]]. He joins a massive, multi-disciplinary ritual circle alongside various [[Archmagi]] and the [[Zenith Processor]] to perform a high-stakes magical operation. The ritual successfully uses [[Benri]]'s [[Mirror of Silence]] and the collective power of the participants to manifest and capture a copy of the [[Iron King]] within an [[Aethertech]] device, signaling a major victory for [[Solstice]].}}
* '''Chapter 39:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|After the [[Zenith Processor]] and [[Solstice]] secure a victory over the [[Iron Legion]], [[Benri Vallenar]] attempts to leverage his connection to the new [[Iron King]], only to be confronted by the [[World Spirits]] of Fathom and [[Zykaros]]. The spirits massacre the gathered dignitaries, forcing [[Benri]]'s clone to flee in a starship before being killed by Fathom's projection. The real [[Benri]] receives the clone's memories and realizes [[Solstice]] is backed by [[World Spirits]], but he is ultimately assassinated by a mole within his own corporation before being revived in stasis.}}
* '''Chapter 40:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|After using pathfinding magic{{!}}pathfinding spells to trace the connections of [[Solstice]], the crew discovers that the organization's leadership is likely located in the [[Zykaros system]]. During a meeting to plan their descent, [[Omega]] tricks the crew with a fake moment of vulnerability before being officially sworn into the group. As the Captain and [[Horizon]] attempt to scout the planet using magic, they discover that [[Zykaros]] is shrouded in darkness by a [[World Spirit]] and detect the presence of both the [[Iron King]]'s magic and a familiar pink fire.}}
* '''Chapter 41:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Set 108 years in the past on the moon of Fathom, [[Omega]] confronts a dying President [[Telappen Vashei]] amidst the ruins of a battle. Despite [[Omega]]'s insistence that he has defeated her and destroyed the organization known as [[Solstice]], Vashei mocks his understanding of the true power structure. The chapter concludes with Vashei dying before she can reveal who is actually in control, leaving [[Omega]] screaming in frustration at her corpse.}}
* '''Chapter 42:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The narrator, [[Sola]], and [[Omega]] arrive on the toxic planet [[Zykaros]], where [[Omega]] uses a private detective agency owned by the [[Escalon Company]] to hunt for their enemies. A heated confrontation ensues between [[Omega]] and his grandson, [[Jak]], the company's president, regarding [[Omega]]'s reckless plan to bait [[Solstice]]. The chapter concludes as [[Sola]] detects the target's location, but realizes that Planetary [[Security]] has also been alerted to their presence.}}
* '''Chapter 43:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|After escaping an assassination attempt on her apartment, [[Lutoch Terelle]] is rescued by a maintenance vehicle and guided by the [[Zenith Processor]] to the heavily fortified [[Center for Arcane Research]]. Despite activating a subspace shield to isolate the facility, [[Varic Vallenar]] uses powerful magic to breach the building and capture [[Lutoch]] using hypnotic eyes. The chapter concludes as [[Aila Escalon]] arrives with the [[Zenith Blade]], breaking Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic's mental hold on [[Lutoch]].}}
* '''Chapter 44:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|After a fierce duel with [[Aila Escalon]], Varic Vallenar{{!}}Captain Vallenar uses a pre-planned bombardment from his starship to escape the collapsing building with a high-value prisoner. He is intercepted by [[Zykaros]], the [[World Spirit]], who reveals that the organization [[Solstice]] is actually composed of [[World Spirits]]. Realizing they cannot win against such entities, Vallenar's ally [[Sola]] uses a temporal field to extract him from the battlefield, prompting his decision to retreat.}}
* '''Chapter 45:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|While Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax manages her fleet's advance into the [[Zykaros system]], a traitor on her flagship deactivates the subspace defenses and allows an enemy clone named [[Aila Escalon]] to board. Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax and her brother [[Veshran]] fight a desperate battle against the intruder, eventually destroying [[Aila]]'s physical form but causing catastrophic damage to the ship's bridge. As the hull breaches, [[Veshran]] suddenly turns on Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax and stabs her, before she is blown out into the vacuum of space.}}
* '''Chapter 46:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Raion]] battles [[Aila Escalon]] in his failing [[Red Divine Titan]], but after a mechanical failure and an intervention by [[Hallow Divide]], he is forced to eject from the mech. Meanwhile, [[Mell]] attempts to defend her ship using the [[Starhammer]] android but is intercepted by [[Void Prism]], who hijacks her consciousness via a [[Subspace]] feed. As [[Mell]] becomes mesmerized by the subspace patterns, [[Void Prism]] prepares to steal her android.}}
* '''Chapter 47:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|After realizing his lifelong quest to destroy [[Solstice]] was futile, [[Omega]] feigns surrender to his daughter [[Aila]] before attempting a dangerous escape through a complex [[Subspace lock]]. He successfully reaches his ship, [[The Last Horizon]], and uses his remaining organic mass to perform a desperate, life-saving regeneration on the dying [[Commander]] Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax. This act of self-sacrifice stabilizes the ship's crew but results in [[Omega]]'s own light fading as he exhausts his life force.}}
* '''Chapter 48:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|After his starship is shot down and his companion [[Omega]] is killed, the Captain is confronted by the [[World Spirit]] [[Zykaros]], who demands he join her mission to unify the galaxy. During a chaotic battle involving an emerald blade and a sudden elemental flood, a more powerful [[World Spirit]] named Fathom arrives and destroys the Captain's staff. To prevent total destruction, the Captain is forced to enter a [[binding]] contract with Fathom and [[Zykaros]], inadvertently triggering a catastrophic release of curse magic.}}
* '''Chapter 49:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|As the starship [[The Last Horizon]] undergoes a strange, lightning-fueled transformation due to the Captain's changing state, [[Sola]] calls upon [[Shadow Ark]] for an emergency evacuation. Meanwhile, a character uses seventh magic to cast a corrosive curse on the [[World Spirit]] Fathom, inadvertently infecting the entire galaxy and causing global chaos. Amidst this destruction, a mysterious Shadow{{!}}shadow entity strikes a deal with the [[Zenith Chamber]] to cooperate in exchange for specific prizes, leaving the galaxy without any remaining opponents.}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Horizon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Sola]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Queen Shyrax]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aila]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Varic Vallenar]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Mell]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Raion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Void Prism]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Benri]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Iron King]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Starhammer]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aila Escalon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Hallow Divide]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Voice of Solstice]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Nila]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omal]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Benri Vallenar]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Teranon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Mariala]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Prism]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Veshran]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Melerius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Felrex]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Nera Jesh]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Doctor Cryce]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Herta]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Prince Veshran]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Captain (The Last Horizon)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Jak]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Iron Bishop]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Lemon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Lutoch Terelle]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Parryl]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Vice Chief of Planetary Security]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Processor]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Divide]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega’s daughter]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Gunder]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Mariala Brechess]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[King Regent]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Visiri]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Grave Hound]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Leilari]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Divine Titans]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Mell D-One-Twenty-Five]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[First Director of the Magic Tower]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Doctor Oladyne]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Doctor Elios]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Kerathin]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Solstice]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Galactic Union]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Fathom (The Last Horizon)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Zykaros]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Subspace]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Iron Legion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Karoshan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Vallenar Corporation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[D’Niss]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Karosha]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Perfected]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Magic Tower]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Subline]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Union]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Dornoth system]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Planetary Security (The Last Horizon)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Visiria]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Zenith Colony]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Dornoth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Escalon Company]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Ark]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Fathom Planetary Security]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Advocates]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Universal Manufacturing]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Dornoth IV]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Grand Hive]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Dornoth III]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Karoshan Alliance]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Karoshan fleet]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Zenith Chamber]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Basyrryx]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Lumen Arkana]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Fathom system]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Huun]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Zykaros system]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Center for Arcane Research]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Mirror of Silence]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[pathfinding magic]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aetheric symbols]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Combat Art]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Terminus Mundi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Temporal Field]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[contract magic]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|pathfinding spell}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Combat Artist]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|sealing magic}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Absolute Burial]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Subspace warp]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Last Horizon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Zenith Processor]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Zenith Starship]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Zenith Blade]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Zenith Device]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Nova-Bots]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Zenith Devices]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Eurias]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Divine Titan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Subspace Drive]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Red Divine Titan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|data core}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[force-blade]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Blade]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Edict]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[levitation ring]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|shield amulet}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Lightcaster IV]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Mortal Edict]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aether]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Lichborn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aethertech]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[World Spirit]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Oracle of Solstice]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Fallen Sword]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Pilot]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Engineer]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[D’Niss invasion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Keeper virus]]}}

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
